sourceMovie.title: TMNT
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto TMNT (2007) as a adventure theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The adventure and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Big Hero 6
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The adventure energy of TMNT is alive in Big Hero 6 (2014), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a funny payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: Despicable Me 3
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Despicable Me 3 (2017) operates in the same adventure as TMNT, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The funny payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Lego Ninjago Movie
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Lego Ninjago Movie (2017) belongs to the same conversation as TMNT — both anchor their adventure in animation emotion that doesn't let go. The funny undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: Phineas and Ferb The Movie: Across the 2nd Dimension
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If TMNT's adventure stayed with you, Phineas and Ferb The Movie: Across the 2nd Dimension (2011) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The animation through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where TMNT excels in adventure, Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1990) doubles down on comedy to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles II: The Secret of the Ooze
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles II: The Secret of the Ooze (1991) is the natural next step after TMNT: same adventure DNA, different perspective. The funny streak and comedy elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Secret Headquarters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of TMNT who respond to its adventure will find Secret Headquarters (2022) immediately familiar. Both films share a comedy sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason TMNT fans keep landing on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Mutant Mayhem (2023) — the animation is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Aquaman and the Lost Kingdom draws from the same adventure well as TMNT, channeling action in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2023 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Shall We Dance?
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Shall We Dance? (2004) captures the comedy appeal of TMNT without simply imitating it. The drama streak running through both films is what puts Shall We Dance? at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

faq.question: What should I watch after TMNT?
faq.answer: Try Big Hero 6, Despicable Me 3, The Lego Ninjago Movie—each shares DNA with TMNT while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is TMNT?
faq.answer: TMNT (2007) is best described as Adventure, Animation, Comedy, Family. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is TMNT worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
